---
title: "simulation draft"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Multi-Level Simulation & Bootstrap Framework
# Forensic Examiner — Inconclusive / Error Model
# =============================================================================
# Requires: lme4, dplyr, tidyr
#
# Model fitted on bullets_2024.xlsx — Primary (Baseline) phase
# fit_inc : Inconclusive ~ Difficulty + Quality1 + Quality2 + (1|AnonID) + (1|Cset)
# fit_err : Error        ~ Difficulty + Quality1 + Quality2 + (1|AnonID)
#           (bobyqa optimizer; VEasy collapsed into Easy to resolve separation)
#
# Difficulty factor order used in per_diff: VEasy, Easy, Norm, Diff, VDiff
#   reference level = "Diff"  (coefficient = 0)
#
# Quality1 levels (3 in fit_inc): A(ref), B, C
# Quality2 levels (4 in fit_inc): A(ref), B, C, D
# =============================================================================

library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0.  Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
inv_logit  <- function(x) 1 / (1 + exp(-x))
clamp_prob <- function(p, lo = 1e-6, hi = 1 - 1e-6) pmax(lo, pmin(hi, p))

# Factor level labels (used to label the simulated dataset)
DIFF_LEVELS  <- c("VEasy", "Easy", "Norm", "Diff", "VDiff")
QUAL1_LEVELS <- c("A", "B", "C")
QUAL2_LEVELS <- c("A", "B", "C", "D")
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 1.  one_simulation()
# =============================================================================
#
# per_diff       : length-5 proportions for [VEasy, Easy, Norm, Diff, VDiff]
# per_exam_skill : length-3 proportions for examiner skill [Low, Med, High]
#                  (skill shifts the error linear predictor via beta_err$skill)
# per_quality1   : length-3 proportions for Quality1 [A, B, C]
# per_quality2   : length-4 proportions for Quality2 [A, B, C, D]
# Hawthorne      : scalar multiplied by beta_inc$hawthorne / beta_err$hawthorne
# n_examiners    : number of synthetic examiners
# nKQ / nQQ      : number of csets of each comparison type
# mated_prop     : fraction of csets that are Mated
# beta_inc       : named list — fixed effects for Inconclusive model
# sd_exam_inc    : AnonID SD from fit_inc  (fitted = 1.1677)
# sd_cset_inc    : Cset   SD from fit_inc  (fitted = 0.8605)
# beta_err       : named list — fixed effects for Error model
# sd_exam_err    : AnonID SD from fit_err  (replace with your fitted value)
# sd_cset_err    : Cset   SD for error model (0 if (1|Cset) not in fit_err)
# seed           : RNG seed (NULL = not set inside function)
# =============================================================================
one_simulation <- function(

    # --- Simulation design inputs -------------------------------------------
    per_diff        = rep(0.2, 5),
    per_exam_skill  = rep(1/3, 3),
    per_quality1    = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),
    per_quality2    = rep(0.25, 4),
    Hawthorne       = 0,
    n_examiners     = 30,
    nKQ             = 50,
    nQQ             = 50,
    mated_prop      = 0.5,
    seed            = NULL,

    # --- Inconclusive model: defaults = your fitted values from fit_inc ------
    beta_inc = list(
      # Intercept: log-odds of inconclusive at Diff / Q1=A / Q2=A, no Hawthorne
      intercept =  0.1495,

      # Difficulty offsets (order: VEasy, Easy, Norm, Diff[ref=0], VDiff)
      diff      = c(-17.2028, -5.0423, -1.1706, 0.0000, 0.9113),

      # Examiner skill offsets (Low[ref=0], Med, High)
      # Not estimated in fit_inc — set to 0 or override to add a skill effect
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),

      # Quality1 offsets (A[ref=0], B, C)
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.5377, 1.6007),

      # Quality2 offsets (A[ref=0], B, C, D)
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.2857, 0.2219, 0.6841),

      # Hawthorne coefficient (alpha_H); multiplied by the Hawthorne argument
      hawthorne = 0.5
    ),
    sd_exam_inc = 1.1677,   # AnonID SD from fit_inc VarCorr
    sd_cset_inc = 0.8605,   # Cset   SD from fit_inc VarCorr

    # --- Error model: fitted from fit_err (bobyqa, responses2, no (1|Cset)) ---
    beta_err = list(
      # Intercept: log-odds of error at Diff / Q1=A / Q2=A
      intercept = -2.2259,

      # Difficulty offsets (order: VEasy, Easy, Norm, Diff[ref=0], VDiff)
      # VEasy collapsed into Easy during model fitting (zero errors in VEasy);
      # VEasy is assigned Easy's coefficient as the conservative default.
      diff      = c(-3.0829, -3.0829, -0.6228, 0.0000, 0.5032),
      #              VEasy    Easy     Norm     Diff    VDiff

      # Examiner skill offsets — not in fit_err, kept at 0
      # Override to add a skill effect: e.g. c(0.4, 0.0, -0.4) for Low/Med/High
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),

      # Quality1 offsets (A[ref=0], B, C)
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.1779, 1.4058),

      # Quality2 offsets (A[ref=0], B, C, D)
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.6122, 0.5528, 0.7438),

      hawthorne = 0.0
    ),
    sd_exam_err = 0.9655,  # AnonID SD from fit_err VarCorr
    sd_cset_err = 0.0      # (1|Cset) not included in fit_err

) {

  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)

  # ---- validate inputs -------------------------------------------------------
  stopifnot(
    length(per_diff)       == 5,
    length(per_exam_skill) == 3,
    length(per_quality1)   == length(beta_inc$quality1),
    length(per_quality2)   == length(beta_inc$quality2),
    abs(sum(per_diff)       - 1) < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(per_exam_skill) - 1) < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(per_quality1)   - 1) < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(per_quality2)   - 1) < 1e-8
  )

  n_csets <- nKQ + nQQ

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 1 — Simulate examiners
  # ==========================================================================
  examiners <- tibble(
    AnonID   = paste0("E", sprintf("%03d", seq_len(n_examiners))),
    SkillIdx = sample(1:3, n_examiners, replace = TRUE, prob = per_exam_skill),
    u_inc    = rnorm(n_examiners, 0, sd_exam_inc),
    u_err    = rnorm(n_examiners, 0, sd_exam_err)
  )

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 2 — Simulate csets
  # ==========================================================================
  csets <- tibble(
    Cset       = paste0("C", sprintf("%04d", seq_len(n_csets))),
    Comparison = c(rep("KQ", nKQ), rep("QQ", nQQ)),
    Mating     = sample(c("Mated", "Nonmated"), n_csets, replace = TRUE,
                        prob = c(mated_prop, 1 - mated_prop)),
    DiffIdx    = sample(1:5, n_csets, replace = TRUE, prob = per_diff),
    Qual1Idx   = sample(seq_along(per_quality1), n_csets, replace = TRUE,
                        prob = per_quality1),
    Qual2Idx   = sample(seq_along(per_quality2), n_csets, replace = TRUE,
                        prob = per_quality2),
    Difficulty = DIFF_LEVELS[DiffIdx],
    Quality1   = QUAL1_LEVELS[Qual1Idx],
    Quality2   = QUAL2_LEVELS[Qual2Idx],
    v_inc      = rnorm(n_csets, 0, sd_cset_inc),
    v_err      = rnorm(n_csets, 0, sd_cset_err)
  )

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 3 — Full crossed design  (every examiner sees every cset)
  # ==========================================================================
  sim_data <- tidyr::crossing(
    select(examiners, AnonID, SkillIdx, u_inc, u_err),
    select(csets, Cset, Comparison, Mating,
           DiffIdx, Qual1Idx, Qual2Idx,
           Difficulty, Quality1, Quality2,
           v_inc, v_err)
  )

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 4 — Inconclusive outcome
  # ==========================================================================
  sim_data <- sim_data %>%
    mutate(
      lp_inc =
        beta_inc$intercept             +
        beta_inc$diff[DiffIdx]         +
        beta_inc$skill[SkillIdx]       +
        beta_inc$quality1[Qual1Idx]    +
        beta_inc$quality2[Qual2Idx]    +
        beta_inc$hawthorne * Hawthorne +
        u_inc + v_inc,

      p_inc        = clamp_prob(inv_logit(lp_inc)),
      Inconclusive = rbinom(n(), 1, p_inc)
    )

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 5 — Error outcome  (conclusive rows only)
  # ==========================================================================
  # NOTE: dplyr::if_else() evaluates BOTH branches for all rows before
  # selecting, so passing p_err (NA for inconclusive rows) into rbinom()
  # produces NAs. We compute lp_err/p_err/Error only on conclusive rows
  # using a row index instead.
  sim_data <- sim_data %>%
    mutate(lp_err = NA_real_, p_err = NA_real_, Error = NA_integer_)

  conclusive_idx <- which(sim_data$Inconclusive == 0)

  sim_data$lp_err[conclusive_idx] <-
    beta_err$intercept +
    beta_err$diff[    sim_data$DiffIdx[ conclusive_idx]] +
    beta_err$skill[   sim_data$SkillIdx[conclusive_idx]] +
    beta_err$quality1[sim_data$Qual1Idx[conclusive_idx]] +
    beta_err$quality2[sim_data$Qual2Idx[conclusive_idx]] +
    beta_err$hawthorne * Hawthorne +
    sim_data$u_err[conclusive_idx] +
    sim_data$v_err[conclusive_idx]

  sim_data$p_err[conclusive_idx] <-
    clamp_prob(inv_logit(sim_data$lp_err[conclusive_idx]))

  sim_data$Error[conclusive_idx] <-
    rbinom(length(conclusive_idx), 1, sim_data$p_err[conclusive_idx])

  # ==========================================================================
  # Step 6 — Reconstruct Decision (mirrors real data coding)
  # ==========================================================================
  sim_data %>%
    mutate(
      Decision_D = case_when(
        Inconclusive == 1                        ~ "Insuff",
        Error == 0 & Mating == "Mated"           ~ "ID",
        Error == 0 & Mating == "Nonmated"        ~ "Excl",
        Error == 1 & Mating == "Mated"           ~ "Excl",    # false exclusion
        Error == 1 & Mating == "Nonmated"        ~ "ID",      # false ID
        TRUE                                     ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 2.  bootstrap_statistic()
#
#  Computes inc_rate and err_rate at three levels of aggregation:
#
#  OVERALL  : single rates across the whole dataset
#  BY ROW   : per Difficulty x Mating cell (row-level breakdown)
#  BY EXAMINER : one rate per examiner (AnonID)
#  BY CSET     : one rate per cset (Cset)
#
#  Returns a named list. The by_* elements are tibbles with one row per
#  group — these can be summarised across bootstrap replicates to get
#  group-specific CIs.
# =============================================================================
bootstrap_statistic <- function(df, decision_col = "Decision_D") {

  dec     <- df[[decision_col]]
  n_total <- nrow(df)
  n_inc   <- sum(dec == "Insuff",   na.rm = TRUE)
  n_conc  <- sum(dec != "Insuff" & !is.na(dec))
  conc_df <- df[!is.na(dec) & dec != "Insuff", ]
  n_err   <- sum(conc_df$Error == 1, na.rm = TRUE)

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Overall rates
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  overall <- list(
    inc_rate = n_inc / n_total,
    err_rate = if (n_conc > 0) n_err / n_conc else NA_real_,
    n_total  = n_total,
    n_inc    = n_inc,
    n_conc   = n_conc,
    n_err    = n_err
  )

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # By row  — breakdown by Difficulty x Mating cell
  # Each cell gets its own inc_rate and err_rate.
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  by_row <- df %>%
    mutate(.dec = .data[[decision_col]]) %>%
    group_by(Difficulty, Mating) %>%
    summarise(
      n_total  = n(),
      n_inc    = sum(.dec == "Insuff",  na.rm = TRUE),
      n_conc   = sum(.dec != "Insuff" & !is.na(.dec)),
      n_err    = sum(Error == 1 & .dec != "Insuff", na.rm = TRUE),
      inc_rate = n_inc / n_total,
      err_rate = if_else(n_conc > 0, n_err / n_conc, NA_real_),
      .groups  = "drop"
    )

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # By examiner  — one inc_rate and err_rate per AnonID
  # Useful for detecting which examiners drive coverage failures.
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  by_examiner <- df %>%
    mutate(.dec = .data[[decision_col]]) %>%
    group_by(AnonID) %>%
    summarise(
      n_total  = n(),
      n_inc    = sum(.dec == "Insuff",  na.rm = TRUE),
      n_conc   = sum(.dec != "Insuff" & !is.na(.dec)),
      n_err    = sum(Error == 1 & .dec != "Insuff", na.rm = TRUE),
      inc_rate = n_inc / n_total,
      err_rate = if_else(n_conc > 0, n_err / n_conc, NA_real_),
      .groups  = "drop"
    )

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # By cset  — one inc_rate and err_rate per Cset
  # Useful for detecting which csets are systematically hard.
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  by_cset <- df %>%
    mutate(.dec = .data[[decision_col]]) %>%
    group_by(Cset, Difficulty, Mating) %>%
    summarise(
      n_total  = n(),
      n_inc    = sum(.dec == "Insuff",  na.rm = TRUE),
      n_conc   = sum(.dec != "Insuff" & !is.na(.dec)),
      n_err    = sum(Error == 1 & .dec != "Insuff", na.rm = TRUE),
      inc_rate = n_inc / n_total,
      err_rate = if_else(n_conc > 0, n_err / n_conc, NA_real_),
      .groups  = "drop"
    )

  list(
    overall      = overall,
    by_row       = by_row,
    by_examiner  = by_examiner,
    by_cset      = by_cset
  )
}





# =============================================================================
# 5.  true_parameters()
#
#  Computes the TRUE inc_rate and err_rate for a given scenario by averaging
#  over the fixed effects and random effect distributions analytically.
#  This is the "ground truth" the bootstrap CIs are evaluated against.
#
#  Because the logit link is nonlinear, E[inv_logit(lp)] != inv_logit(E[lp]).
#  We approximate via Monte Carlo integration over the random effects.
# =============================================================================
true_parameters <- function(
    per_diff       = rep(0.2, 5),
    per_exam_skill = rep(1/3, 3),
    per_quality1   = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),
    per_quality2   = rep(0.25, 4),
    Hawthorne      = 0,
    mated_prop     = 0.5,
    # Defaults mirror one_simulation() — written out explicitly to avoid
    # lazy-eval hangs from formals() references
    beta_inc = list(
      intercept =  0.1495,
      diff      = c(-17.2028, -5.0423, -1.1706, 0.0000, 0.9113),
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.5377, 1.6007),
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.2857, 0.2219, 0.6841),
      hawthorne = 0.5
    ),
    sd_exam_inc = 1.1677,
    sd_cset_inc = 0.8605,
    beta_err = list(
      intercept = -2.2259,
      diff      = c(-3.0829, -3.0829, -0.6228, 0.0000, 0.5032),
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.1779, 1.4058),
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.6122, 0.5528, 0.7438),
      hawthorne = 0.0
    ),
    sd_exam_err = 0.9655,
    sd_cset_err = 0.0,
    n_mc        = 50000
) {

  # Draw random effects for MC integration
  u_inc <- rnorm(n_mc, 0, sd_exam_inc)
  v_inc <- rnorm(n_mc, 0, sd_cset_inc)
  u_err <- rnorm(n_mc, 0, sd_exam_err)
  v_err <- rnorm(n_mc, 0, sd_cset_err)

  # Expected fixed-effect contribution: weighted average over design proportions
  fe_inc <- sum(per_diff       * beta_inc$diff)     +
            sum(per_exam_skill * beta_inc$skill)    +
            sum(per_quality1   * beta_inc$quality1) +
            sum(per_quality2   * beta_inc$quality2) +
            beta_inc$intercept                      +
            beta_inc$hawthorne * Hawthorne

  fe_err <- sum(per_diff       * beta_err$diff)     +
            sum(per_exam_skill * beta_err$skill)    +
            sum(per_quality1   * beta_err$quality1) +
            sum(per_quality2   * beta_err$quality2) +
            beta_err$intercept                      +
            beta_err$hawthorne * Hawthorne

  # True inconclusive rate: E[p_inc] averaged over random effects
  true_inc <- mean(inv_logit(fe_inc + u_inc + v_inc))

  # True error rate: E[p_err | conclusive], weighted by mated_prop.
  # An error occurs when:
  #   - cset is Mated    (prob = mated_prop)     and examiner calls Excl
  #   - cset is Nonmated (prob = 1 - mated_prop) and examiner calls ID
  # p_err from fit_err is the probability of making a wrong conclusive
  # decision. mated_prop scales how often each error type can occur but
  # since fit_err pools both error types, mated_prop enters as a weight
  # on the conclusive pool rather than changing the linear predictor.
  p_inc_mc <- inv_logit(fe_inc + u_inc + v_inc)
  p_err_mc <- inv_logit(fe_err + u_err + v_err)

  # Weight conclusive draws by mated_prop: mated and nonmated csets both
  # contribute to the conclusive pool, each with probability (1 - p_inc).
  # The overall error rate among conclusives is the same regardless of
  # mated_prop because fit_err does not include a Mating term — but we
  # carry mated_prop through so the function signature matches one_simulation()
  # and Sim_boot_cov() can pass scenario_args to both without filtering.
  true_err <- sum(p_err_mc * (1 - p_inc_mc)) / sum(1 - p_inc_mc)

  list(
    true_inc_rate = true_inc,
    true_err_rate = true_err,
    mated_prop    = mated_prop    # echoed back for reference
  )
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 3.  simulate_coverage()
#
#  Single unified function replacing simulate_and_bootstrap() and
#  coverage_simulation(). All scenario arguments are specified once.
#
#  WHAT IT DOES
#  ------------
#  1. Computes TRUE inc_rate / err_rate via Monte Carlo integration.
#  2. Repeats M times:
#       a. Generate one synthetic dataset with one_simulation()
#       b. Run B bootstrap replicates
#       c. Build a percentile CI and record whether it covers the true value
#  3. Returns coverage rates, per-repetition CIs, and (optionally) the last
#     simulated dataset + bootstrap replicates for inspection.
#
#  ARGUMENTS
#  ---------
#  M                : outer repetitions (how many datasets to generate)
#  B                : bootstrap replicates per repetition
#  bootstrap_method : "row" | "examiner" | "cset"
#  conf_level       : nominal coverage level (default 0.95)
#  decision_col     : name of Decision column produced by one_simulation()
#  seed             : master seed; repetition m uses seed + m internally
#  keep_last        : if TRUE, attach the last repetition's full bootstrap
#                     replicates to the return value (useful for diagnostics)
#  ...              : ALL scenario arguments forwarded to one_simulation()
#                     (per_diff, per_exam_skill, per_quality1, per_quality2,
#                      Hawthorne, n_examiners, nKQ, nQQ, mated_prop,
#                      beta_inc, sd_exam_inc, sd_cset_inc,
#                      beta_err, sd_exam_err, sd_cset_err)
# =============================================================================
simulate_coverage <- function(
    M                = 200,
    B                = 500,
    bootstrap_method = c("row", "examiner", "cset"),
    conf_level       = 0.95,
    decision_col     = "Decision_D",
    seed             = 42,
    keep_last        = FALSE,
    ...
) {

  bootstrap_method <- match.arg(bootstrap_method)
  alpha <- 1 - conf_level
  dots  <- list(...)

  # Split ... into two arg lists:
  #   true_par_args  accepted by true_parameters() — no n_examiners/nKQ/nQQ
  #   sim_args       full set for one_simulation()
  sc_sim_only   <- c("n_examiners", "nKQ", "nQQ")
  true_par_args <- dots[!names(dots) %in% sc_sim_only]
  sim_args      <- dots

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 1: compute true parameters for this scenario
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  message("Computing true parameters via Monte Carlo integration...")
  true_par <- do.call(true_parameters, true_par_args)
  message(sprintf(
    "  True inc_rate = %.4f  |  True err_rate = %.4f",
    true_par$true_inc_rate, true_par$true_err_rate
  ))

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 2: outer loop — M repetitions
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  message(sprintf(
    "Running %d repetitions x %d bootstrap replicates [method = '%s']...",
    M, B, bootstrap_method
  ))

  results   <- vector("list", M)
  last_boot <- NULL

  for (m in seq_len(M)) {

    if (m %% 25 == 0) message(sprintf("  repetition %d / %d", m, M))

    set.seed(seed + m)

    # --- 2a. Generate one synthetic dataset ----------------------------------
    sim_df   <- do.call(one_simulation, sim_args)
    obs_full <- bootstrap_statistic(sim_df, decision_col)
    obs_stat <- obs_full$overall   # scalar rates for coverage check

    # --- 2b. Bootstrap loop --------------------------------------------------
    boot_stats <- vector("list", B)

    for (b in seq_len(B)) {

      boot_df <- switch(bootstrap_method,

        "row" = {
          sim_df[sample(nrow(sim_df), nrow(sim_df), replace = TRUE), ]
        },

        "examiner" = {
          exams   <- unique(sim_df$AnonID)
          sampled <- sample(exams, length(exams), replace = TRUE)
          lapply(seq_along(sampled), function(i) {
            sim_df %>%
              filter(AnonID == sampled[i]) %>%
              mutate(AnonID = paste0(AnonID, "_b", i))
          }) %>% bind_rows()
        },

        "cset" = {
          cs      <- unique(sim_df$Cset)
          sampled <- sample(cs, length(cs), replace = TRUE)
          lapply(seq_along(sampled), function(i) {
            sim_df %>%
              filter(Cset == sampled[i]) %>%
              mutate(Cset = paste0(Cset, "_b", i))
          }) %>% bind_rows()
        }
      )

      s <- bootstrap_statistic(boot_df, decision_col)

      # Overall scalar stats — used for coverage CI
      boot_stats[[b]] <- tibble(
        inc_rate = s$overall$inc_rate,
        err_rate = s$overall$err_rate,
        n_total  = s$overall$n_total,
        n_inc    = s$overall$n_inc,
        n_conc   = s$overall$n_conc,
        n_err    = s$overall$n_err,

        # By-examiner mean rates (scalar summaries across examiners)
        inc_rate_exam_mean = mean(s$by_examiner$inc_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
        err_rate_exam_mean = mean(s$by_examiner$err_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
        inc_rate_exam_sd   = sd(s$by_examiner$inc_rate,   na.rm = TRUE),
        err_rate_exam_sd   = sd(s$by_examiner$err_rate,   na.rm = TRUE),

        # By-cset mean rates (scalar summaries across csets)
        inc_rate_cset_mean = mean(s$by_cset$inc_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
        err_rate_cset_mean = mean(s$by_cset$err_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
        inc_rate_cset_sd   = sd(s$by_cset$inc_rate,   na.rm = TRUE),
        err_rate_cset_sd   = sd(s$by_cset$err_rate,   na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    }

    boot_tbl <- bind_rows(boot_stats)

    # --- 2c. Percentile CI and coverage check (overall rates) ----------------
    ci_wide <- boot_tbl %>%
      summarise(
        inc_lo = quantile(inc_rate, alpha / 2,     na.rm = TRUE),
        inc_hi = quantile(inc_rate, 1 - alpha / 2, na.rm = TRUE),
        err_lo = quantile(err_rate, alpha / 2,     na.rm = TRUE),
        err_hi = quantile(err_rate, 1 - alpha / 2, na.rm = TRUE)
      )

    results[[m]] <- tibble(
      rep          = m,
      obs_inc_rate = obs_stat$inc_rate,
      obs_err_rate = obs_stat$err_rate,
      inc_lo       = ci_wide$inc_lo,
      inc_hi       = ci_wide$inc_hi,
      err_lo       = ci_wide$err_lo,
      err_hi       = ci_wide$err_hi,
      inc_covered  = (true_par$true_inc_rate >= ci_wide$inc_lo) &
                     (true_par$true_inc_rate <= ci_wide$inc_hi),
      err_covered  = (true_par$true_err_rate >= ci_wide$err_lo) &
                     (true_par$true_err_rate <= ci_wide$err_hi)
    )

    if (keep_last   && m == M) last_boot <- boot_tbl
    if (return_data && m == M) last_sim  <- sim_df
  }

  rep_tbl <- bind_rows(results)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 3: coverage summary
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  coverage_summary <- tibble(
    statistic        = c("inc_rate", "err_rate"),
    true_value       = c(true_par$true_inc_rate, true_par$true_err_rate),
    coverage_rate    = c(mean(rep_tbl$inc_covered, na.rm = TRUE),
                         mean(rep_tbl$err_covered, na.rm = TRUE)),
    mean_ci_width    = c(mean(rep_tbl$inc_hi - rep_tbl$inc_lo, na.rm = TRUE),
                         mean(rep_tbl$err_hi - rep_tbl$err_lo, na.rm = TRUE)),
    nominal_coverage = conf_level,
    M                = M,
    B                = B,
    bootstrap_method = bootstrap_method
  )

  message("\n=== Coverage Summary ===")
  print(coverage_summary)

  out <- list(
    coverage_summary = coverage_summary,
    repetitions      = rep_tbl,
    true_parameters  = true_par,
    bootstrap_method = bootstrap_method,
    M                = M,
    B                = B,
    seed             = seed
  )
  if (keep_last)   out$last_boot_replicates <- last_boot
  if (return_data) out$simulated_data       <- last_sim
  out
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 4.  compare_coverage_scenarios()
#
#  Run simulate_coverage() across a named list of scenarios and return
#  a tidy table comparing coverage rates side by side.
#
# ARGUMENTS
# ---------
# scenarios : named list; each element is a list of scenario-specific args
#             that OVERRIDE the shared args below.
#             e.g. list(
#               baseline     = list(per_diff = rep(0.2, 5), Hawthorne = 0),
#               hard_csets   = list(per_diff = c(0.05,0.10,0.20,0.40,0.25)),
#               hawthorne_on = list(Hawthorne = 1)
#             )
# ...       : shared arguments passed to every call of simulate_coverage()
#             (M, B, bootstrap_method, seed, n_examiners, per_exam_skill, etc.)
# =============================================================================
compare_coverage_scenarios <- function(scenarios, ...) {

  shared_args <- list(...)

  all_results <- lapply(names(scenarios), function(nm) {
    message(sprintf("\n========== Scenario: %s ==========", nm))
    # scenario-specific args override shared args
    args <- modifyList(shared_args, scenarios[[nm]])
    res  <- do.call(simulate_coverage, args)
    res$coverage_summary %>% mutate(scenario = nm, .before = 1)
  })

  coverage_table <- bind_rows(all_results)
  message("\n=== All Scenarios Coverage Table ===")
  print(coverage_table)
  coverage_table
}


# =============================================================================
# 4b. extract_var_prop()
#     Computes variance partition from a fitted glmer object.
#     Uses pi^2/3 as the fixed residual variance on the logit scale.
# =============================================================================
extract_var_prop <- function(fit) {
  vc   <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(fit))
  vars <- setNames(vc$vcov, vc$grp)
  v_exam <- if ("AnonID" %in% names(vars)) vars["AnonID"] else 0
  v_cset <- if ("Cset"   %in% names(vars)) vars["Cset"]   else 0
  v_res  <- pi^2 / 3
  v_tot  <- v_exam + v_cset + v_res
  tibble(
    var_exam   = v_exam,         var_cset   = v_cset,
    var_resid  = v_res,          prop_exam  = v_exam / v_tot,
    prop_cset  = v_cset / v_tot, prop_resid = v_res  / v_tot
  )
}


# =============================================================================
# 4c. fit_models_once()
#     Refits fit_inc and fit_err on one simulated dataset.
#     BUG FIX: fit_err collapses VEasy into Easy before fitting, mirroring the
#     real responses2 preprocessing. Without this, VEasy cells frequently have
#     zero errors in small datasets causing separation and NA variance estimates.
# =============================================================================
fit_models_once <- function(sim_df) {

  fit_inc <- tryCatch(
    glmer(
      Inconclusive ~ Difficulty + Quality1 + Quality2 + (1 | AnonID) + (1 | Cset),
      data    = sim_df,
      family  = binomial,
      control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  # Collapse VEasy into Easy — mirrors responses2 preprocessing.
  sim_df_err <- sim_df %>%
    filter(Inconclusive == 0) %>%
    mutate(
      Difficulty = as.character(Difficulty),
      Difficulty = if_else(Difficulty == "VEasy", "Easy", Difficulty),
      Difficulty = factor(Difficulty, levels = c("Easy", "Norm", "Diff", "VDiff"))
    )

  fit_err <- tryCatch(
    glmer(
      Error ~ Difficulty + Quality1 + Quality2 + (1 | AnonID),
      data    = sim_df_err,
      family  = binomial,
      control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  na_var <- tibble(
    var_exam = NA_real_, var_cset = NA_real_, var_resid = NA_real_,
    prop_exam = NA_real_, prop_cset = NA_real_, prop_resid = NA_real_
  )

  list(
    fit_inc = fit_inc,
    fit_err = fit_err,
    inc_var = if (!is.null(fit_inc)) extract_var_prop(fit_inc) else na_var,
    err_var = if (!is.null(fit_err)) extract_var_prop(fit_err) else na_var
  )
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 5.  Sim_boot_cov()
#
#  Single entry-point wrapper. Every parameter is explicit — no ... needed.
#  Internally calls:
#    true_parameters()    to get the ground-truth inc/err rates
#    one_simulation()     to generate one synthetic dataset per repetition
#    bootstrap_statistic() to summarise each bootstrap resample
#  and assembles coverage results exactly as simulate_coverage() does.
#
#  SCENARIO PARAMETERS (passed to one_simulation + true_parameters)
#  -----------------------------------------------------------------
#  per_diff        : proportions for [VEasy, Easy, Norm, Diff, VDiff]
#  per_exam_skill  : proportions for [Low, Med, High] skill
#  per_quality1    : proportions for Quality1 [A, B, C]
#  per_quality2    : proportions for Quality2 [A, B, C, D]
#  Hawthorne       : scalar Hawthorne multiplier (0 = off)
#  n_examiners     : number of synthetic examiners per dataset
#  nKQ / nQQ       : number of KQ / QQ csets per dataset
#  mated_prop      : fraction of csets that are Mated
#  beta_inc        : named list of fixed effects for inconclusive model
#  sd_exam_inc     : examiner SD for inconclusive model
#  sd_cset_inc     : cset SD for inconclusive model
#  beta_err        : named list of fixed effects for error model
#  sd_exam_err     : examiner SD for error model
#  sd_cset_err     : cset SD for error model
#
#  BOOTSTRAP / COVERAGE PARAMETERS
#  --------------------------------
#  M                : outer repetitions (datasets generated)
#  B                : bootstrap replicates per repetition
#  bootstrap_method : "row" | "examiner" | "cset"
#  conf_level       : nominal coverage (default 0.95)
#  decision_col     : name of Decision column
#  seed             : master RNG seed
#  keep_last        : if TRUE, attach last repetition's boot_replicates
#  return_data      : if TRUE, attach last repetition's simulated dataset
#                     (use with M = 1, B = 2000 as a quick diagnostic peek)
# =============================================================================
Sim_boot_cov <- function(

    # --- Scenario parameters -------------------------------------------------
    per_diff        = rep(0.2, 5),
    per_exam_skill  = rep(1/3, 3),
    per_quality1    = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),
    per_quality2    = rep(0.25, 4),
    Hawthorne       = 0,
    n_examiners     = 30,
    nKQ             = 50,
    nQQ             = 50,
    mated_prop      = 0.5,

    beta_inc = list(
      intercept =  0.1495,
      diff      = c(-17.2028, -5.0423, -1.1706, 0.0000, 0.9113),
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.5377, 1.6007),
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.2857, 0.2219, 0.6841),
      hawthorne = 0.5
    ),
    sd_exam_inc = 1.1677,
    sd_cset_inc = 0.8605,

    beta_err = list(
      intercept = -2.2259,
      diff      = c(-3.0829, -3.0829, -0.6228, 0.0000, 0.5032),
      skill     = c(0, 0, 0),
      quality1  = c(0.0000, 0.1779, 1.4058),
      quality2  = c(0.0000, 0.6122, 0.5528, 0.7438),
      hawthorne = 0.0
    ),
    sd_exam_err = 0.9655,
    sd_cset_err = 0.0,

    # --- Bootstrap / coverage parameters ------------------------------------
    M                = 200,
    B                = 500,
    bootstrap_method = c("row", "examiner", "cset"),
    conf_level       = 0.95,
    decision_col     = "Decision_D",
    seed             = 42,
    keep_last        = FALSE,
    return_data      = FALSE   # M=1, B=2000 gives a quick diagnostic peek

) {

  bootstrap_method <- match.arg(bootstrap_method)
  alpha <- 1 - conf_level

  # Args shared by true_parameters() — model + design proportions only
  # (true_parameters does not generate data, so n_examiners/nKQ/nQQ/mated_prop
  #  are excluded from this list)
  true_par_args <- list(
    per_diff       = per_diff,
    per_exam_skill = per_exam_skill,
    per_quality1   = per_quality1,
    per_quality2   = per_quality2,
    Hawthorne      = Hawthorne,
    mated_prop     = mated_prop,
    beta_inc       = beta_inc,
    sd_exam_inc    = sd_exam_inc,
    sd_cset_inc    = sd_cset_inc,
    beta_err       = beta_err,
    sd_exam_err    = sd_exam_err,
    sd_cset_err    = sd_cset_err
  )

  # Args for one_simulation() — all of the above plus data-generation sizes
  sim_args <- c(true_par_args, list(
    n_examiners = n_examiners,
    nKQ         = nKQ,
    nQQ         = nQQ
  ))

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 1: ground-truth rates for this scenario
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  message("Computing true parameters via Monte Carlo integration...")
  true_par <- do.call(true_parameters, true_par_args)
  message(sprintf(
    "  True inc_rate = %.4f  |  True err_rate = %.4f",
    true_par$true_inc_rate, true_par$true_err_rate
  ))

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 2: M repetitions — simulate + bootstrap + check coverage
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  message(sprintf(
    "Running %d repetitions x %d bootstrap replicates [method = '%s']...",
    M, B, bootstrap_method
  ))

  results   <- vector("list", M)
  last_boot <- NULL

  for (m in seq_len(M)) {

    if (m %% 25 == 0) message(sprintf("  repetition %d / %d", m, M))

    set.seed(seed + m)

    # 2a. Generate one synthetic dataset
    sim_df   <- do.call(one_simulation, sim_args)
    obs_stat <- bootstrap_statistic(sim_df, decision_col)$overall

    # 2b. Bootstrap loop
    boot_stats <- vector("list", B)

    for (b in seq_len(B)) {

      boot_df <- switch(bootstrap_method,

        "row" = {
          sim_df[sample(nrow(sim_df), nrow(sim_df), replace = TRUE), ]
        },

        "examiner" = {
          exams   <- unique(sim_df$AnonID)
          sampled <- sample(exams, length(exams), replace = TRUE)
          lapply(seq_along(sampled), function(i) {
            sim_df %>%
              filter(AnonID == sampled[i]) %>%
              mutate(AnonID = paste0(AnonID, "_b", i))
          }) %>% bind_rows()
        },

        "cset" = {
          cs      <- unique(sim_df$Cset)
          sampled <- sample(cs, length(cs), replace = TRUE)
          lapply(seq_along(sampled), function(i) {
            sim_df %>%
              filter(Cset == sampled[i]) %>%
              mutate(Cset = paste0(Cset, "_b", i))
          }) %>% bind_rows()
        }
      )

      s <- bootstrap_statistic(boot_df, decision_col)$overall
      boot_stats[[b]] <- tibble(
        inc_rate = s$inc_rate,
        err_rate = s$err_rate,
        n_total  = s$n_total,
        n_inc    = s$n_inc,
        n_conc   = s$n_conc,
        n_err    = s$n_err
      )
    }

    boot_tbl <- bind_rows(boot_stats)

    # 2c. Percentile CI and coverage check
    ci_wide <- boot_tbl %>%
      summarise(
        inc_lo = quantile(inc_rate, alpha / 2,     na.rm = TRUE),
        inc_hi = quantile(inc_rate, 1 - alpha / 2, na.rm = TRUE),
        err_lo = quantile(err_rate, alpha / 2,     na.rm = TRUE),
        err_hi = quantile(err_rate, 1 - alpha / 2, na.rm = TRUE)
      )

    results[[m]] <- tibble(
      rep          = m,
      obs_inc_rate = obs_stat$inc_rate,
      obs_err_rate = obs_stat$err_rate,
      inc_lo       = ci_wide$inc_lo,
      inc_hi       = ci_wide$inc_hi,
      err_lo       = ci_wide$err_lo,
      err_hi       = ci_wide$err_hi,
      inc_covered  = (true_par$true_inc_rate >= ci_wide$inc_lo) &
                     (true_par$true_inc_rate <= ci_wide$inc_hi),
      err_covered  = (true_par$true_err_rate >= ci_wide$err_lo) &
                     (true_par$true_err_rate <= ci_wide$err_hi)
    )

    if (keep_last && m == M) last_boot <- boot_tbl
  }

  rep_tbl <- bind_rows(results)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 3: coverage summary
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  coverage_summary <- tibble(
    statistic        = c("inc_rate", "err_rate"),
    true_value       = c(true_par$true_inc_rate, true_par$true_err_rate),
    coverage_rate    = c(mean(rep_tbl$inc_covered, na.rm = TRUE),
                         mean(rep_tbl$err_covered, na.rm = TRUE)),
    mean_ci_width    = c(mean(rep_tbl$inc_hi - rep_tbl$inc_lo, na.rm = TRUE),
                         mean(rep_tbl$err_hi - rep_tbl$err_lo, na.rm = TRUE)),
    nominal_coverage = conf_level,
    M                = M,
    B                = B,
    bootstrap_method = bootstrap_method
  )

  message("\n=== Coverage Summary ===")
  print(coverage_summary)

  out <- list(
    coverage_summary = coverage_summary,
    repetitions      = rep_tbl,
    true_parameters  = true_par,
    bootstrap_method = bootstrap_method,
    M                = M,
    B                = B,
    seed             = seed
  )
  if (keep_last) out$last_boot_replicates <- last_boot
  out
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 5.  Example usage
# =============================================================================

# --- A) Single scenario ------------------------------------------------------
# result <- simulate_coverage(
#   M                = 200,
#   B                = 500,
#   bootstrap_method = "examiner",   # recommended: examiner SD = 1.17 dominates
#   seed             = 42,
#   per_diff         = rep(0.2, 5),
#   per_exam_skill   = rep(1/3, 3),
#   per_quality1     = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),
#   per_quality2     = rep(0.25, 4),
#   Hawthorne        = 0,
#   n_examiners      = 49,
#   nKQ              = 788,
#   nQQ              = 793
# )
#
# result$coverage_summary
# #  statistic  true_value  coverage_rate  mean_ci_width  nominal_coverage
# #  inc_rate   0.xxx       0.94           0.0xx          0.95
# #  err_rate   0.xxx       0.93           0.0xx          0.95
#
# # Inspect per-repetition CIs (each row = one synthetic dataset)
# head(result$repetitions)
#
# # Plot CI width distribution
# hist(result$repetitions$inc_hi - result$repetitions$inc_lo,
#      main = "CI width across repetitions: inc_rate", xlab = "Width")


# --- B) Compare scenarios side by side ---------------------------------------
# scenarios <- list(
#   baseline     = list(per_diff = rep(0.2, 5),              Hawthorne = 0),
#   hard_csets   = list(per_diff = c(0.05, 0.10, 0.20, 0.40, 0.25)),
#   hawthorne_on = list(Hawthorne = 1)
# )
#
# # Shared args apply to ALL scenarios; scenario list overrides where specified
# coverage_table <- compare_coverage_scenarios(
#   scenarios        = scenarios,
#   M                = 200,
#   B                = 500,
#   bootstrap_method = "examiner",
#   seed             = 42,
#   per_diff         = rep(0.2, 5),    # default; overridden in hard_csets
#   per_exam_skill   = rep(1/3, 3),
#   per_quality1     = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),
#   per_quality2     = rep(0.25, 4),
#   Hawthorne        = 0,              # default; overridden in hawthorne_on
#   n_examiners      = 49,
#   nKQ              = 788,
#   nQQ              = 793
# )
#
# # Reading the table:
# #   coverage_rate ≈ 0.95  → bootstrap well-calibrated for this scenario
# #   coverage_rate << 0.95  → CIs too narrow (undercovers)
# #   coverage_rate >> 0.95  → CIs too wide   (overcovers)
```
